questions.question: Kaggle provides free GPU compute of approximately:
questions.explanation: 30 GPU hours/week resets weekly. Internet must be enabled (Settings). Best for training small models or inference with large ones.

questions.question: Stratified K-fold cross-validation in NLP competitions ensures:
options: Label distribution is preserved in each fold -- critical for imbalanced datasets
questions.explanation: Random split risks all positives going to one fold. Stratified split: each fold has same class proportion as full dataset.

questions.question: Out-of-fold (OOF) predictions are used to:
options: Evaluate model performance without overfitting to the test set, and for ensemble stacking
questions.explanation: OOF: predictions on val fold from each CV fold. Together they cover all training data -- unbiased estimate of generalisation.

questions.question: Public LB (leaderboard) represents approximately what fraction of test data?
questions.explanation: Public LB: 30% of test. Private LB: 70%, revealed at competition end. Overfitting to public LB often drops rank on private LB.

questions.question: DeBERTa-v3 is popular in Kaggle NLP competitions because:
options: It consistently outperforms BERT and RoBERTa on classification with similar compute
It has the largest context window
questions.explanation: DeBERTa uses disentangled attention + enhanced mask decoder. microsoft/deberta-v3-base is the go-to baseline for NLP classification.

questions.question: Pseudo-labelling in competitions involves:
options: Predicting on test set, adding high-confidence predictions as labelled training data
questions.explanation: Train on labelled data, predict test set, add examples where P(class) > 0.95 as pseudo-labels, retrain. Typically +0.1-0.3% AUC.

questions.question: Ensemble diversity improves results because:
options: Different models/seeds make different errors -- averaging cancels out individual mistakes
questions.explanation: If model A makes correct predictions on cases model B gets wrong, averaging them outperforms either alone. Diversity is the key.

questions.question: Data leakage in NLP competitions occurs when:
options: Future information accidentally appears in training features, inflating CV scores
questions.explanation: Leakage examples: using the submission file to get test labels, features derived from the full dataset before train/test split.

questions.question: Why use max_seq_length = 95th percentile of token count, not 100th?
options: The top 5% of long documents add computation cost disproportionate to their information gain
questions.explanation: Truncating outliers at 95th percentile: 5% of examples slightly truncated, but 2-5x speedup from shorter average sequence. Accuracy impact minimal.

questions.question: After a competition, the most valuable learning comes from:
options: Reading top-ranked solution write-ups in the discussion forum
questions.explanation: Top solution discussions reveal: feature engineering insights, model choices, ensembling strategy, what didn't work. Essential for levelling up.
